evidence: Import-time side effects exist in shared modules: `examples/a2a_gateway/01_basic_gateway.py` calls `setup_logging()` at module import, and `tests/support/integration/config.py` constructs `test_settings = TestSettings()` at module import.

Test runtime/link wiring is duplicated across modules (`tests/runtime/test_presence.py`, `tests/runtime/test_runtime.py`, `tests/platform/test_link_contacts.py`) instead of using one shared boundary fixture/fake for link transport behavior.

Configuration ownership is split across parallel loaders: `examples/common/bootstrap.py` resolves runtime config, `docker/shared/config_loader.py` reimplements YAML+credential merge logic, and `examples/coding_agents/test_communication.py` adds a third YAML config path.

Config-loading logic is split between `docker/shared/config_loader.py` and a second local implementation in `examples/claude_sdk_docker/runner.py`, with near-duplicate YAML/error handling and slightly different config-shaping behavior.

Sibling integration `__init__.py` files expose inconsistent public surfaces: some re-export concrete symbols (`src/thenvoi/integrations/langgraph/__init__.py`, `src/thenvoi/integrations/claude_sdk/__init__.py`, `src/thenvoi/integrations/a2a/__init__.py`) while others intentionally export nothing with `__all__: list[str] = []` (`src/thenvoi/integrations/anthropic/__init__.py`, `src/thenvoi/integrations/parlant/__init__.py`, `src/thenvoi/integrations/pydantic_ai/__init__.py`).

Example orchestrator modules are pure compatibility shims (`examples/a2a_gateway/demo_orchestrator/*.py`) while package-level orchestrator exports also exist in `src/thenvoi/integrations/a2a_gateway/orchestrator/__init__.py`, creating layered but uneven organization expectations across sibling examples.

`src/thenvoi/integrations/langgraph/graph_tools.py` explicitly lets subgraph errors bubble to callers, while `src/thenvoi/integrations/parlant/tools.py` catches broad exceptions in `_run_tool()` and converts failures into `ToolResult(data=...)` strings.

Runner/config paths normalize YAML/read failures to `ValueError` (`docker/shared/config_loader.py`, `examples/claude_sdk_docker/runner.py`), but prompt loading in runners (`Path.read_text()` in `docker/claude_sdk/runner.py` and `examples/claude_sdk_docker/runner.py`) is not normalized and can leak raw filesystem exceptions.
Example tool flows therefore expose mixed failure contracts in adjacent integration paths (raise vs string error payload), making caller-level handling less predictable.

`src/thenvoi/runtime/tools.py:269-329` defines a centralized `TOOL_MODELS`/tool-name taxonomy, but multiple adapters rebuild parallel tool abstractions instead of consuming one shared registration pipeline.
`src/thenvoi/adapters/pydantic_ai.py:136-356` manually defines and registers many near-identical tool wrapper functions that only translate arguments into `invoke_platform_tool(...)` calls.
`src/thenvoi/adapters/claude_sdk.py:107-159` hardcodes `_MCP_TOOL_SCHEMAS` in parallel to runtime tool models, creating a second contract surface for the same tools.
`src/thenvoi/adapters/crewai.py:100-251` defines a separate per-tool input-model matrix (`CrewAI*Input` + `CREWAI_TOOL_SCHEMAS`) that duplicates the same tool API surface again.

Peer-resolution and routing abstractions are duplicated across gateway layers: `src/thenvoi/integrations/a2a/gateway/adapter.py:231-245` and `src/thenvoi/integrations/a2a/gateway/server.py:73-93` both implement slug/UUID resolution logic.

`src/thenvoi/runtime/contacts/contact_handler.py:55-62` and `src/thenvoi/runtime/platform_runtime.py:229-247` coordinate via multiple untyped callback channels (`on_broadcast`, `on_hub_event`, `on_hub_init`), creating an implicit protocol rather than a single explicit boundary.

`src/thenvoi/integrations/a2a_gateway/orchestrator/agent.py` contains core routing behavior (`stream`, `_aget_agent_response`, `close`) but current tests target executor behavior and a compatibility import path (`tests/example_agents/a2a_gateway/test_orchestrator_executor.py`, `tests/example_agents/a2a_gateway/test_orchestrator_context.py`).

`tests/example_agents/a2a_gateway/test_orchestrator_context.py` imports `make_call_peer_agent_tool` from `examples/a2a_gateway/demo_orchestrator/agent.py` (shim), not the canonical module under `src/thenvoi/integrations/a2a_gateway/orchestrator/agent.py`, creating indirection in API-surface verification.

`src/thenvoi/runtime/contacts/service.py` has explicit failure branches when REST calls return no `data` (raises `RuntimeError`), while `tests/runtime/test_contact_tools.py` exercises happy paths through a wrapper and does not cover these error branches.

`examples/common/bootstrap.py` and `examples/prompts/characters.py` are reused by many examples (high importer count in packet context) but have no dedicated tests validating env fallback failure modes or prompt contract stability.

`src/thenvoi/runtime/types.py` defines runtime-facing message/config APIs (`PlatformMessage`, `normalize_handle`, contact synthetic constants), while many composition tests validate `thenvoi.core.types.PlatformMessage` (`tests/core/test_composition_types.py`), leaving runtime-type behavior and consistency weakly guarded.

The runtime package keeps legacy entry points `src/thenvoi/runtime/contact_handler.py` and `src/thenvoi/runtime/contact_service.py` as compatibility shims while canonical implementations live under `src/thenvoi/runtime/contacts/`, creating two valid locations for the same contact domain.

`tests/runtime/test_broadcast_changes.py` imports `thenvoi.runtime.contact_handler` (shim path), while production wiring in `src/thenvoi/runtime/platform_runtime.py` (from holistic context) imports `thenvoi.runtime.contacts.contact_handler`, showing split import affinity for one subsystem.

Holistic structure signals show flat overload in `tests/runtime/` (16 files), and assigned files `tests/runtime/test_broadcast_changes.py` and `tests/runtime/test_contact_config.py` are contact-domain tests that are not colocated with a contact-focused subfolder.

`src/thenvoi/runtime/__init__.py` exposes a broad barrel surface across multiple concerns (execution, tools, prompts, trackers, shutdown) but contact domain is separately nested under `runtime/contacts`, which weakens directory boundary clarity for runtime subdomains.

Character/demo example scripts repeat nearly identical startup orchestration (load env, read credentials, construct adapter, create Agent, run) across multiple files, e.g. examples/anthropic/03_tom_agent.py, examples/claude_sdk/03_tom_agent.py, examples/crewai/05_tom_agent.py, examples/langgraph/07_tom_agent.py.

Several example files mutate import paths at module import time (`sys.path.insert(...)`) to compensate for layout coupling, e.g. examples/anthropic/04_jerry_agent.py, examples/claude_sdk/04_jerry_agent.py, examples/langgraph/07_tom_agent.py, examples/pydantic_ai/04_jerry_agent.py.

Large orchestration modules mix configuration parsing, lifecycle/reconnect loops, participant caching, event consumption, deduplication, and routing in one place, especially src/thenvoi/integrations/a2a_bridge/bridge.py and src/thenvoi/runtime/contacts/contact_handler.py.

Runner entrypoints duplicate the same config-load/prompt-assembly/tool-load/startup pipeline with drift across docker/claude_sdk/runner.py, examples/claude_sdk_docker/runner.py, and docker/codex/runner.py.

Compatibility shims use inconsistent migration patterns (deprecation-on-access vs silent re-export), e.g. src/thenvoi/adapters/a2a.py and src/thenvoi/adapters/a2a_gateway.py vs thenvoi-bridge/bridge_core/*.py and src/thenvoi/runtime/contact_handler.py.

Several modules contain tutorial-style, multi-screen docstrings and inline comments that restate obvious code mechanics (for example `src/thenvoi/integrations/langgraph/graph_tools.py` lines 23-122 and 132-192).
Compatibility shims in `examples/a2a_gateway/demo_orchestrator/*.py` are pure re-export pass-through wrappers with no policy/translation logic (`agent.py`, `agent_executor.py`, `remote_agent.py`).
Eight `examples/*/setup_logging.py` files repeat the same 3-line pattern, changing only a string argument, indicating copy-pasted ceremony rather than a single declarative mapping.

findings.summary: Shared modules perform startup work at import time, creating order-dependent coupling.
evidence: `examples/a2a_gateway/01_basic_gateway.py` executes `setup_logging()` at module scope, so importing the module mutates process logging configuration.
`tests/support/integration/config.py` creates `test_settings = TestSettings()` at module scope, binding environment/file-derived config during import rather than fixture/runtime boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Move import-time initialization behind explicit functions/fixtures: call logging setup only inside `main()`, and replace module-global `test_settings` with a fixture or cached accessor initialized on first use.

findings.summary: Link/WebSocket contract mocking is duplicated across tests instead of centralized.
evidence: `tests/runtime/test_presence.py` and `tests/runtime/test_runtime.py` each build large custom `mock_link` fixtures with overlapping async method stubs and iterator behavior.
`tests/platform/test_link_contacts.py` separately patches `WebSocketClient` and defines another transport contract mock pattern.
The same transport/event boundary is recreated in multiple files, increasing blast radius when link API shape changes.
findings.suggestion: Create a shared test boundary layer (e.g., `tests/support/fakes_link.py` + shared fixtures in `conftest.py`) that provides canonical `ThenvoiLink`/WebSocket test doubles; keep per-test customization minimal via overrides.

findings.summary: Runtime config and credential resolution are fragmented into parallel loaders.
evidence: `examples/common/bootstrap.py` implements env + agent config resolution (`load_platform_urls`, `load_runtime_config`).
`docker/shared/config_loader.py` separately parses YAML and injects credentials with its own merge semantics.
`examples/coding_agents/test_communication.py` adds another ad-hoc YAML loader (`load_agent_config`) that bypasses shared config-loading contracts.
findings.suggestion: Define one shared config-resolution module for scripts/examples (environment defaults, YAML parsing, credential injection, error policy) and have docker/example utilities depend on that boundary rather than re-implementing local loaders.

findings.summary: Runner config loading convention diverged into duplicated implementations.
evidence: `docker/shared/config_loader.py` and `examples/claude_sdk_docker/runner.py:load_config()` both perform near-identical YAML parsing and ValueError wrapping.
The shared loader supports keyed sections (`agent_key`) and copies either section/global config into a new dict, while example runner hardcodes `agent` and mutates the loaded config in place.
`docker/claude_sdk/runner.py` uses the shared loader, but example runner keeps a local variant, creating two conventions for the same responsibility.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate runner config loading to one shared function (prefer `docker/shared/config_loader.py`), parameterize key selection for all runners, and remove the duplicate loader in `examples/claude_sdk_docker/runner.py`.

findings.summary: Sibling integration packages use conflicting export conventions.
evidence: Some integration packages expose utility symbols via `__all__` and direct imports (langgraph, claude_sdk, a2a), while others publish empty surfaces (`__all__: list[str] = []`).
This is not just naming variation; it changes whether sibling packages behave like importable facades or documentation-only placeholders.
The pattern forces users to remember package-specific import rules instead of relying on one integration package convention.
findings.suggestion: Define and enforce a single integration package policy (facade exports or internal-only modules), document it, then align all `src/thenvoi/integrations/*/__init__.py` files to that policy.

findings.summary: Tool integrations mix exception bubbling and in-band string errors.
evidence: `graph_as_tool()` states and implements bubble-up behavior for subgraph errors (`await graph.ainvoke(...)` without catch).
`parlant/tools.py` catches broad `Exception` in `_run_tool()` and returns `ToolResult(data=format_tool_error(...))`, converting failures into normal tool return values.

`examples/langgraph/standalone_calculator.py` raises `ValueError` for invalid operations/divide-by-zero, which propagates through the LangGraph path but would be converted to string payloads in the Parlant path.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one cross-integration failure contract (typed exception mapping or structured error result object) and implement adapters that normalize errors before returning to framework-specific tool layers.

findings.summary: Runner prompt-file reads bypass the loaders’ normalized error strategy.
evidence: Both runners normalize config read/parse failures via ValueError in loader code paths.
Both runners read role prompt files with `prompt_file.read_text(...)` without local exception normalization.
A missing/unreadable prompt file in an existing path context can surface raw `OSError` while nearby config errors are wrapped uniformly, producing inconsistent boundary behavior.
findings.suggestion: Wrap prompt-file reads in the same normalization strategy used for config loading (e.g., catch `OSError` and raise a typed config/runtime setup error with path context).

findings.summary: Tool API abstraction is re-implemented per adapter, creating parallel contracts and drift risk.
evidence: `runtime/tools.py` already centralizes tool names/input models (`TOOL_MODELS`), but `pydantic_ai.py` hand-writes many wrapper funcs that differ mostly by tool name and arg dict.
`claude_sdk.py` maintains separate `_MCP_TOOL_SCHEMAS` with overlapping fields for the same tool set.
`crewai.py` introduces a third schema layer (`CrewAI*Input` + `CREWAI_TOOL_SCHEMAS`) for the same platform tools.
Tests in `test_pydantic_ai_adapter.py` assert tool-by-tool registration by name, reinforcing duplicated per-adapter surfaces instead of validating one shared source.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single adapter-agnostic `ToolBindingRegistry` derived from `TOOL_MODELS` that can emit framework-specific bindings (PydanticAI function tools, CrewAI BaseTool classes, Claude MCP schemas). Keep framework glue thin and generated from the shared registry to remove manual per-tool wrappers.

findings.summary: A2A gateway duplicates peer-identity resolution logic across server and adapter layers.
evidence: `A2AGatewayAdapter._resolve_peer` and `GatewayServer._resolve_peer` both implement slug-first/UUID-fallback lookup over parallel peer maps.
Both classes carry `peers` + `peers_by_uuid` state and repeat fallback behavior, increasing maintenance overhead when identifier semantics change.
Integration tests exercise adapter internals (`_get_or_create_room`, peer map mutation) rather than a single boundary object, indicating abstractions are split and leaking.
findings.suggestion: Extract peer identity resolution into one shared component (e.g., `PeerDirectory`) used by both `GatewayServer` and `A2AGatewayAdapter`; pass resolved `PeerRef` objects through callback boundaries instead of repeating lookup rules in each layer.

findings.summary: Contact hub orchestration relies on loosely coupled callbacks instead of an explicit integration interface.
evidence: `ContactEventHandler` takes three optional callbacks (`on_broadcast`, `on_hub_event`, `on_hub_init`) and infers behavior from which are set.
`PlatformRuntime._setup_contact_handling` wires those callbacks and coordinates extra state (`_pending_hub_room_id`, queued broadcasts), forming an implicit cross-class protocol.
Tests validate internal sequencing/state transitions (hub readiness, pending broadcasts), which indicates behavior depends on fragile handshake details instead of a single explicit abstraction.
findings.suggestion: Replace callback triad with a typed `ContactEventSink` interface (broadcast, hub-init, hub-inject semantics in one contract) and move readiness/pending-state ownership into one coordinator object to reduce implicit coupling.

findings.summary: Example runner path uses layered pass-through wrappers with minimal policy, increasing indirection cost.
evidence: `examples/run_agent.py` defines many `_invoke_*` functions that mostly forward argument bags to `run_*` functions.
`example_runners.py` then defines multiple `run_*_agent` functions that mostly instantiate adapter, call `Agent.create(...)`, and `await agent.run()` with small variations.
The two-layer dispatch adds many function surfaces with little abstraction gain, making behavior changes require touching many thin wrappers.
findings.suggestion: Collapse the wrapper chain into a data-driven runner registry: declarative per-framework adapter factory + optional feature hooks (contacts/streaming/thinking), then one generic invoke path that constructs and runs the agent.

findings.summary: Canonical orchestrator agent logic is not directly tested, leaving key routing/status branches unverified.
evidence: `agent.py` implements `stream()` and `_aget_agent_response()` with multiple status mappings (`input_required`, `error`, `completed`) and fallback behavior.
Current orchestrator tests focus on executor outcomes and tool context propagation; they do not exercise `_aget_agent_response` branch matrix or `stream` emission sequencing in the canonical class.
A regression in status mapping or fallback content can pass existing tests while breaking API behavior seen by A2A clients.
findings.suggestion: Add direct unit tests for `OrchestratorAgent` with a mocked LangGraph state/stream to cover each status mapping branch, unknown structured response fallback, interim tool-call event emission, and `close()` behavior.

findings.summary: Contact service failure-mode branches are untested because tests validate wrapper happy paths only.
evidence: `ContactService.add_contact`, `remove_contact`, and `respond_contact_request` raise `RuntimeError` when `response.data` is missing.
`tests/runtime/test_contact_tools.py` exercises `ContactTools` with mocked successful responses and input validation, but does not assert behavior when downstream API returns empty/partial payloads.
This leaves critical API failure translation and caller-facing error semantics unprotected.
findings.suggestion: Add service-level tests (or wrapper tests with explicit empty `response.data`) that assert raised exceptions and messages for each failure branch, including list metadata fallbacks when metadata is absent.

findings.summary: High-reuse example bootstrap and prompt-building APIs have no regression tests despite being shared entry points.
evidence: `bootstrap.py` encapsulates env loading and required URL validation used across many examples, but no tests were found for missing env/default precedence or `Agent.create` argument wiring.
`characters.py` generates long instruction templates consumed by multiple example integrations, but there are no tests guarding required behavioral constraints in generated prompts.
Given broad reuse, minor edits can silently break many examples without detection.
findings.suggestion: Add lightweight tests for bootstrap config resolution (env/default/error branches) and snapshot/contract tests for prompt builders that assert presence of required sections/constraints without over-coupling to full text.

findings.summary: Type/API coverage is split between core and runtime message models, leaving runtime-specific behavior under-tested.
evidence: `runtime/types.py` defines `PlatformMessage.format_for_llm`, `normalize_handle`, and contact synthetic constants used by runtime flows.

`tests/core/test_composition_types.py` validates `core.types.PlatformMessage` behavior (including fallback to `Unknown`), not runtime `PlatformMessage`, which has different implementation assumptions.
This dual-model setup increases risk that tests pass while runtime formatting/normalization behavior diverges from expected API semantics.
findings.suggestion: Add dedicated tests for `thenvoi.runtime.types` (including `normalize_handle` edge cases and runtime `PlatformMessage.format_for_llm`) and document/verify intended parity or intentional divergence from `core.types`.

findings.summary: Contact runtime functionality is split between canonical package and root-level compatibility shims.
evidence: `src/thenvoi/runtime/contact_handler.py` and `src/thenvoi/runtime/contact_service.py` contain only re-exports to `thenvoi.runtime.contacts.*` but still provide first-class import paths.
Tests continue importing the shim path (`from thenvoi.runtime.contact_handler import ContactEventHandler`) instead of canonical `thenvoi.runtime.contacts.contact_handler`.
This creates import-affinity drift inside one domain: both legacy and canonical paths are active, obscuring ownership boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Stage reorg in three steps: (1) create `src/thenvoi/runtime/compat/` and move shim modules there (`contact_handler.py`, `contact_service.py`) to make compatibility explicit; (2) update internal/test imports to canonical paths first (`thenvoi.runtime.contacts.contact_handler` / `.service`) using `rg -n "thenvoi\.runtime\.contact_(handler|service)" src tests`; (3) keep temporary forwarding imports only in `runtime/compat` and remove root-level shims after one release. Validate with `uv run pytest tests/runtime/test_broadcast_changes.py tests/runtime/test_contact_config.py -q` and re-check imports with `rg -n "thenvoi\.runtime\.contact_(handler|service)" src tests`.

findings.summary: Contact-domain runtime tests are stored in a flat runtime test folder instead of a contact-focused subtree.
evidence: Both assigned runtime tests target contact-event behavior/config but live directly under `tests/runtime/`.
Holistic structure reports `tests/runtime/` has 16 files, exceeding the flat-directory overload threshold and mixing multiple runtime concerns.
Because contact code is now explicitly nested under `src/thenvoi/runtime/contacts/`, flat test placement weakens navigability and owner mapping.
findings.suggestion: Stage reorg: (1) create `tests/runtime/contacts/` with `__init__.py`; (2) move `test_broadcast_changes.py` then `test_contact_config.py` into that folder; (3) normalize imports in moved files to canonical contact paths and keep only test-layer fixtures in parent runtime folder. Run `uv run pytest tests/runtime/contacts -q` and `uv run pytest tests/runtime -q` to validate no discovery regressions.

findings.summary: Large `runtime/__init__.py` barrel blends multiple runtime concerns and weakens package boundary discoverability.
evidence: `src/thenvoi/runtime/__init__.py` re-exports many symbols spanning execution, tools, formatting, prompts, trackers, and shutdown.
At the same time, contact functionality is split into dedicated subpackage plus root shim files, so the top-level package surface no longer maps cleanly to directory ownership.
New contributors can discover multiple valid entry points (`runtime.*`, `runtime.contacts.*`, shim modules), increasing boundary ambiguity.
findings.suggestion: Use a staged package-surface reorg: (1) restrict `src/thenvoi/runtime/__init__.py` to stable runtime-core exports only; (2) create explicit subpackage facades (`src/thenvoi/runtime/contacts/__init__.py`) for contact-domain APIs; (3) route legacy imports through `runtime/compat` and document sunset. Validate by running `rg -n "from thenvoi\.runtime import" src tests` to update callers, then `uv run pytest tests/runtime/test_broadcast_changes.py tests/runtime/test_contact_config.py tests/integration/test_hub_room_strategy.py -q`.

findings.summary: README optional-extra name drifts from pyproject dependency contract.
evidence: README.md instructs `uv add "git+https://github.com/thenvoi/thenvoi-sdk-python.git[pydantic_ai]"`.
pyproject.toml declares `[project.optional-dependencies]` key `pydantic-ai`, not `pydantic_ai`.
findings.suggestion: Align the public install contract in one place: either rename the pyproject extra key to `pydantic_ai` for consistency with examples, or update README commands to `pydantic-ai`; then add a lightweight CI/docs check that validates README extras against `[project.optional-dependencies]` keys.

findings.summary: High-fan-in example bootstrap contract is untested despite broad reuse.
evidence: holistic_context marks examples/common/bootstrap.py with fan-in 11 and imports from most example directories.
Search across tests finds no direct coverage for `bootstrap_agent`, `load_runtime_config`, `load_platform_urls`, or `create_agent_from_runtime`.
README positions examples as primary usage paths, making bootstrap behavior part of user-facing contract.
findings.suggestion: Add focused unit tests in `tests/` for bootstrap behavior: env resolution precedence, missing endpoint errors, credential passthrough to `Agent.create`, and dotenv toggle behavior; mock `load_agent_config` and `Agent.create` to keep tests deterministic.

findings.summary: Integration fixture contracts rely on manual invocation instead of enforced default gates.
evidence: tests/support/integration/config.py documents real-server dependency and custom cleanup semantics (`--no-clean`, `.env.test`, real credentials).
pyproject.toml pytest config uses `testpaths = ["tests"]` and `addopts = "-v"` without default integration exclusion/marker gate.
Gating exists in helper decorators (`tests/integration/markers.py`), but enforcement depends on individual test usage rather than central policy.
findings.suggestion: Stage a quality-gate hardening plan: (1) add pytest markers registration (`integration`, `requires_api`, `requires_multi_agent`, `requires_user_api`) and set default `-m "not integration"` in pyproject, (2) explicitly mark integration suites and keep env-based skip decorators as secondary checks, (3) add CI commands `uv run pytest tests/ -m "not integration" -v` and `uv run pytest tests/integration/ -m integration -v` with documented env requirements.

findings.summary: Shared integration markers live in scenario test package instead of support boundary.
evidence: holistic_context lists `tests/integration/markers.py` as a shared utility with 12 importers.
`tests/support/integration/config.py` already hosts reusable integration infra and is imported by integration tests, indicating an existing support boundary.
Keeping shared marker contracts in `tests/integration/` mixes reusable infra with scenario tests and obscures where test governance primitives live.
findings.suggestion: Execute a staged reorg: (1) create `tests/support/integration/markers.py` and move marker definitions there, (2) update imports in integration tests to new path, (3) keep a temporary shim `tests/integration/markers.py` re-exporting from support with deprecation note, (4) remove shim after callers migrate. Validate with `uv run ruff check .`, `uv run pyrefly check`, and `uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/integration/ -v`.

findings.summary: Example agent startup flow is duplicated across frameworks instead of using one composition path.
evidence: Each file repeats the same structural sequence: `load_dotenv()` -> read `THENVOI_WS_URL`/`THENVOI_REST_URL` -> `load_agent_config(...)` -> instantiate framework adapter -> `Agent.create(...)` -> `await agent.run()`.
Differences are mostly adapter class and prompt function, while control flow and error handling are copy-pasted.
The duplication already caused per-file drift (some include KeyboardInterrupt handling and extra logging, others do not).
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared example runner helper that accepts adapter factory + agent key + optional prompt supplier, and migrate these scripts to thin wrappers. Keep framework-specific behavior in small adapter builder functions.

findings.summary: Multiple examples rely on `sys.path` mutation, indicating packaging/layout and execution-mode coupling.
evidence: These files call `sys.path.insert(...)` at import time before loading shared prompt/setup modules.
Sibling scripts in the same example families use package-style imports instead, creating two different execution conventions.
Path mutation pushes environment/bootstrap concerns into every script instead of a coherent package/module entrypoint.
findings.suggestion: Make `examples` consistently importable (package execution via `python -m`/`uv run -m`) and remove `sys.path` mutations. Add a single shared import strategy for prompt/setup modules.

findings.summary: A2A bridge orchestration mixes too many responsibilities in single modules/classes.
evidence: `ThenvoiBridge` in `bridge.py` combines env/config parsing, reconnect policy, signal/shutdown lifecycle, event loop consumption, participant cache management, message deduplication, and dispatch setup.
`MentionRouter.route()` handles filtering, dedup of mentions, lifecycle marking, platform-message normalization, session tracking, fan-out dispatch, and failure collation in one long method.
`ContactEventHandler` similarly combines strategy routing, callback/hub delivery, enrichment, dedup cache, persistence, and broadcast concerns.
findings.suggestion: Split orchestration into focused collaborators: configuration loader, connection supervisor, room/participant cache service, and message dispatch pipeline. Keep top-level classes as coordinators that compose these parts.

findings.summary: Runner entrypoints duplicate setup pipelines, creating maintainability drift across environments.
evidence: Both Claude runners implement near-identical sequences: load config, resolve role prompt files, combine prompt sections, load custom tools, construct adapter, create agent, run with retry, and cleanup.
Codex runner mirrors the same orchestration structure with separate helper parsing, producing parallel but divergent setup code paths.
Shared runner helpers exist (`runner_core.py`) but core composition logic is still repeated in entrypoint modules.
findings.suggestion: Extract a reusable runner composition layer (config normalization + prompt assembly + tool loading + lifecycle wiring) and keep each runner file focused on adapter-specific parameter mapping only.

findings.summary: Compatibility shims use inconsistent migration semantics, obscuring deprecation expectations.
evidence: Adapter shims implement lazy `__getattr__` plus deprecation warnings and explicit removal version.
Bridge/runtime shims are silent direct re-exports with no warning/removal metadata.
Consumers of similarly named legacy modules get different migration behavior depending on subsystem.
findings.suggestion: Standardize shim policy through one shared deprecation shim utility (warning behavior, removal metadata, import forwarding) and apply it uniformly across adapters, bridge_core, and runtime shims.

findings.summary: Excessive tutorial-style docstrings/comments drown signal in core runtime and integration files.
evidence: `graph_as_tool` has a very long didactic docstring and repeated explanatory comments (lines 23-122, 132-192) that mostly narrate straightforward operations already visible in code.
`default_messages_state_formatter` uses an extended example-heavy docstring for a small formatter function (`message_formatters.py` lines 37-65).
`Execution` protocol and module header include migration/tutorial narrative mixed into implementation file (`execution.py` lines 1-13, 61-86), creating high reading overhead before behavior.
Top-level package init docstring (`src/thenvoi/__init__.py` lines 1-41) embeds long examples and architecture narration in import-surface code.
findings.suggestion: Trim these to concise contract-focused docs: keep 1-3 sentence module intent, move tutorials/migration guides to dedicated docs (e.g., `docs/`), and remove comments that only restate code steps.

findings.summary: Demo orchestrator compatibility files are no-op re-export wrappers that add maintenance noise.
evidence: Each shim file imports symbols from canonical module and re-exports via `__all__` without added behavior or boundary policy.
`agent.py` and `agent_executor.py` are effectively import aliases only.
`remote_agent.py` repeats a broad symbol list solely to forward names.
findings.suggestion: Collapse these into a single compatibility entrypoint or remove shims and update callers to canonical imports; if compatibility is required, centralize aliasing in one module with deprecation warnings and sunset timeline.

findings.summary: Example logging setup modules are duplicated boilerplate differing only by namespace string.
evidence: All files contain the same structure: import `build_logging_setup` then assign `setup_logging = build_logging_setup("<name>")`.
Most docstrings are near-identical ('Shared logging configuration for examples' variants), reinforcing copy-template churn.
findings.suggestion: Replace per-framework files with one shared helper that accepts framework id at call site, or generate these aliases from a small registry to avoid repeated handwritten stubs.
